title: luzula nivea subsp. pedemontana
intro: Luzula nivea subsp. pedemontana, also known as Alpine Woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and linear leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Luzula nivea subsp. pedemontana include relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
